title: So is LCL intrinsically primordial soup or not
display_title: So is LCL intrinsically primordial soup or not
wikitext:
[]: So is LCL intrinsically "primordial soup" or not?             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Dec 23, 2006 11:48 pm In episode 20 according to the LTP, Maya states that after Shinji was absorbed into Unit 01, "[t]he LCL ingredients in the plug changed chemically [and] are now similar to the sea water of the primitive earth." Misato then refers to it as "primordial soup". Now, in End of Evangelion, whilst straddling Shinji during Instrumentality, Rei tells Shinji that they're in a sea of LCL, the primordial soup of life. So, is LCL the primordial soup (I had thought that life emerged from the blood of Lilith that was spilled during the DotS), or is it something different that becomes the primordial soup once it has one or more people dissolved into it? It seems kind of ambiguous, as everyone HAD reverted to LCL at that point in EoE if I remember correctly.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sun Dec 24, 2006 12:11 am Red Cross Book wrote: [L.C.L] The liquid that fills the inside of the entry plug. Viscous in nature and having a blood-like odor, when charged by electricity its molecular arrangement is altered, allowing L.C.L to perform a number of functions such as mentally linking the pilot and the Eva, supplying oxygen, and providing both physical and mental protection to the pilot. This same L.C.L also fills the lowest level of Terminal Dogma where Lilith is kept. Various interpretations have been circulated such as that "L.C.L" is the abbreviation of "Link Connected Liquid", but what it appears to be is the actual "soup of life".

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Dec 24, 2006 12:16 am Yes, yes, I know of that, but if it's already the primordial soup, then how does it change INTO the primordial soup once Shinji is absorbed into it?

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sun Dec 24, 2006 12:25 am Misinformation? Hell maybe the LCL needed some kind of kick-start to get evolution started.

[]:             Quote Post by sir_evangelist » Sun Dec 24, 2006 10:10 am The LcL just extracted from lilith is not the primordial soup...bodies where made from here. If shape was created from this lcl, there must be an At Field and consecuently, a soul. A soul is the answer...that was what changed the lcl, because the body alone cannot change nothing(the body is made from the same materials).

[]: Re: So is LCL intrinsically "primordial soup" or n             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Dec 31, 2006 12:47 pm NAveryW wrote: Now, in End of Evangelion, whilst straddling Shinji during Instrumentality, Rei tells Shinji that they're in a sea of LCL, the primordial soup of life.

[]: Re: So is LCL intrinsically "primordial soup" or n             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Sun Dec 31, 2006 1:39 pm Reichu wrote: Regarding Maya's line about chemical alteration -- I've noticed this, too, and I never thought it made any particular sense. If Shinji is made of LCL that isn't the same as 'pure' LCL, how is he able to reembody from the LCL in EVA-01's core?

[]: Re: So is LCL intrinsically "primordial soup" or n             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Dec 31, 2006 1:47 pm MaggotMaster wrote: Because you know, the "ingredients" of Shinji are still there, mixed with the normal LCL from the dummy plug.

[]: Re: So is LCL intrinsically "primordial soup" or n             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Sun Dec 31, 2006 1:50 pm Reichu wrote: MaggotMaster wrote: Because you know, the "ingredients" of Shinji are still there, mixed with the normal LCL from the dummy plug.

[]: Re: So is LCL intrinsically &quot;primordial soup&qu             Quote Post by flatulant » Sun Dec 31, 2006 1:51 pm MaggotMaster wrote: Reichu wrote: MaggotMaster wrote: Because you know, the "ingredients" of Shinji are still there, mixed with the normal LCL from the dummy plug.

[]: Re: So is LCL intrinsically "primordial soup" or n             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Sun Dec 31, 2006 1:53 pm flatulant wrote: MaggotMaster wrote: Reichu wrote: MaggotMaster wrote: Because you know, the "ingredients" of Shinji are still there, mixed with the normal LCL from the dummy plug.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Dec 31, 2006 3:15 pm Eh? No DotS? What did I miss? STUPID CI! YOU CONFUZZLE ME TO NO END!

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sun Dec 31, 2006 4:21 pm NAveryW wrote: Eh? No DotS? What did I miss? STUPID CI! YOU CONFUZZLE ME TO NO END!

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Dec 31, 2006 4:46 pm But I thought the Confidential Information was what started the idea of the DotS in the first place... if it's not from the CI, then from where did it come? /That's right, I avoided ending my sentence with a prepostion. I felt like it.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Dec 31, 2006 4:50 pm I liked the Spears much better when they were entirely servile all-purpose tools that never did anything unexpected. And the entire mythos is infinitely cooler if Adam and Lilith had a bitch-fight that is ritualistically reenacted in the Whi--, er, Black Moon billions of years later (with Adam coming out on the losing side yet again). This is why I am forever lost to fanfic fantasies and a deep-seated resentment of the CI. I hope you're happy, Anno. NAveryW wrote: But I thought the Confidential Information was what started the idea of the DotS in the first place...

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Dec 31, 2006 4:57 pm So the DotS originated in fanfics? P.S. I just thought of something: Is that a vagina on Garak's head?

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sun Dec 31, 2006 4:58 pm Well I got this out of her awhile back over on ANF: Reichu wrote: AEF wrote: Not only were they entertaining most of them made a lot of sense until the CI came along anyways.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Dec 31, 2006 5:06 pm NAveryW wrote: So the DotS originated in fanfics?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Dec 31, 2006 5:45 pm Reichu wrote: P.S. I just thought of something: Is that a vagina on Garak's head?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Dec 31, 2006 10:58 pm Now I need to scour the DS9 DVDs for a "GUL DUKAT IS NOT AMUSED" shot.
